bits.premise: Old-fashioned is polite code for racist.
Diagnoses can become a mnemonic nickname.
Prison coworkers can be worse than inmates.
Warning signs are only useful before sex.

lines.text: How we doing, everybody?
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: -How we doing? Thank you so much.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I'm from the Midwest.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: My family's old-fashioned is what you say
lines.label: setup

lines.text: when your family's still racist.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: And all my friends are starting families of their own,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and one of my friends, his kid, has Asperger's ADHD
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I can never remember a little fucker's name,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: so I just call him "AAA."
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: I used to work at a prison for like three years,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and you meet a lot of interesting people
lines.label: setup

lines.text: when you work at a prison, employees as well.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: We worked with this one guy who would eat his own hair,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: and if you can believe it, he didn't crush it with the women.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: There was reason for that.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: He was into child porn.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: Yeah, he got busted at his house
lines.label: setup

lines.text: with a computer full of child pornography.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: That was the last time I trusted a coworker
lines.label: setup

lines.text: with my laptop.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Thank you.
lines.label: fluff

lines.text: I saw a homeless person outside holding a sign that said,
lines.label: setup

lines.text: "Dying of AIDS."
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Could've used that sign before the blowjob.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: Thank you, guys. I'm Austin Ingalls.
lines.label: fluff
